title: cd — Climate Departure
text: Two products: - reanalysis-era5-land-monthly-means — tmean, prcp, dewpoint, soil moisture (returns zip→GRIB) - derived-era5-land-daily-statistics — tmax, tmin (returns NetCDF, needs day + time_zone params) Key gotchas: - Temperature Kelvin (subtract 273.15) - Precipitation m/day (× 1000 × days_in_month → mm/month) - GRIB metadata lies units (says °C, values K) - retry = 120 avoids rate limiting (default 5 aggressive) - wf_request_batch() parallel requests (20 workers)

MCP works item keys. /zotero-lookup bridges citation keys item data. BBT citation key storage: Feb 2025+, BBT stores citation keys citationKey field directly zotero.sqlite (via Zotero’s item data system), separate BBT database. old better-bibtex.sqlite better-bibtex.migrated files stale longer updated. Query citation keys : SELECT idv.value items JOIN itemData id .itemID = id.itemID JOIN itemDataValues idv id.valueID = idv.valueID JOIN fields f id.fieldID = f.fieldID f.fieldName = 'citationKey'.

title: Climate Departure for the Kootenay Lake Region
text: bundled area interest union four watershed groups (single multi-polygon, EPSG:4326). sf polygon works. watersheds region drain Columbia River — Kootenay Lake feeds Kootenay River joins Columbia Castlegar; Slocan Lower Arrow drain directly Columbia. also carry British Columbia ecoregions analysis. Ecoregions partition province broad climate-physiography zones based latitude, elevation, dominant vegetation. Climate departure can vary across ways regional average hides, use later sub-region per-ecoregion breakdown. Kootenay Lake Region area interest (~24,000 km^2) southern interior British Columbia, coloured ecoregion. Kootenay Lake dominates basin; Lower Arrow Lake Slocan visible west.
code: library(cd) library(sf)  aoi <- st_read(   system.file("extdata", "example_aoi_kootenay_lake.gpkg", package = "cd"),   quiet = TRUE )  area_km2 <- as.numeric(sum(st_area(st_transform(aoi, 3005)))) / 1e6 aoi_bb <- st_bbox(aoi)  ggplot() +   geom_sf(data = ecoregions, aes(fill = name_tc), color = "grey45",           linewidth = 0.3, alpha = 0.45) +   geom_sf(data = aoi, fill = NA, color = "#2166ac", linewidth = 0.8) +   geom_sf(data = lakes, fill = "#a6bddb", color = "#74a9cf", linewidth = 0.2) +   geom_sf(data = rivers, fill = "#a6bddb", color = "#74a9cf", linewidth = 0.2) +   geom_sf(data = streams, color = "#74a9cf", linewidth = 0.3, alpha = 0.6) +   geom_sf(data = highways, color = "#333333", linewidth = 0.5) +   geom_sf(data = towns, color = "black", size = 2.5) +   ggrepel::geom_label_repel(     data = towns,     aes(label = name, geometry = geom),     stat = "sf_coordinates",     size = 3.2, fill = "white", alpha = 0.85,     label.padding = unit(0.2, "lines"),     min.segment.length = 0   ) +   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set3", name = "Ecoregion") +   guides(fill = guide_legend(nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE)) +   coord_sf(     xlim = c(aoi_bb["xmin"] - 0.4, aoi_bb["xmax"] + 0.4),     ylim = c(aoi_bb["ymin"] - 0.5, aoi_bb["ymax"] + 0.3)   ) +   labs(title = "Kootenay Lake Region",        subtitle = paste0(round(area_km2), " km^2 — ", nrow(ecoregions), " ecoregions")) +   theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +   theme(axis.title = element_blank(),         legend.position = "bottom",         legend.text = element_text(size = 8),         legend.title = element_text(size = 9),         legend.key.size = unit(0.4, "cm"))

title: Climate Departure for the Kootenay Lake Region
text: producer pipeline repository fetches ERA5-Land hourly reanalysis data, derives variables (vapour pressure deficit, relative humidity, soil moisture), aggregates monthly / seasonal / annual, writes Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFFs S3. Alongside GeoTIFFs publish static SpatioTemporal Asset Catalog (STAC) — small set JSON files index assets. GeoTIFFs catalog JSON live https://stac-era5-land.s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/catalog.json. cd_catalog() reads URL default. Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFFs also usable directly outside R — example, QGIS via STAC plugin, gdalcubes, STAC-aware client. Available climate variables periods STAC catalog.
code: catalog <- cd_catalog() kableExtra::kable_styling(   knitr::kable(catalog, label = NA,     caption = "Available climate variables and periods in the STAC catalog."),   bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed") ) |>   kableExtra::scroll_box(height = "320px")

title: Climate Departure for the Kootenay Lake Region
text: cd_extract() crops cloud-hosted GeoTIFF area interest computes spatial mean per year. area interest size live extraction takes seconds per variable. keep vignette fast reproducible load pre-computed result exactly call. First 10 rows extracted climate time series.
code: # In a fresh interactive session, you would compute this with: #   ts <- cd_extract(catalog, aoi) # Below we load the pre-computed equivalent so the vignette renders # without hitting the network. ts is the time series tibble; bl_early, # ano, trn, cmp, and cmp_pct are the downstream products. vignette_data <- readRDS(system.file(   "vignette-data", "kootenay_lake.rds", package = "cd" )) ts       <- vignette_data$regional$ts bl_early <- vignette_data$regional$bl ano      <- vignette_data$regional$ano trn      <- vignette_data$regional$trn cmp      <- vignette_data$regional$cmp cmp_pct  <- vignette_data$regional$cmp_pct  knitr::kable(head(ts, 10),   caption = "First 10 rows of the extracted climate time series.")

title: Climate Departure for the Kootenay Lake Region
text: Anomalies computed pre-warming reference period — 1951–1980, three decades climate change accelerated. Saying year “+1.5 °C” means 1.5 °C warmer average year 1951 1980. trend table follows two rows per variable. compute trends two different start years: 1951–present (75 years) — long view. Captures full magnitude warming since pre-warming reference. 1981–present (45 years) — starts beginning World Meteorological Organization’s recent 30-year “climate normal” (1981–2010). reference period used published climate products, makes results easy compare Intergovernmental Panel Climate Change reports government climate summaries. negative = earlier melt)") +   theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +   theme(legend.position = "bottom") get_75 <- function(trn, var) {   trn[trn$variable == var & trn$period == "annual" & trn$trend_start == 1951, ] } rollup <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(results), function(i) {   res <- results[[i]]   cmp <- res$cmp   tmean <- get_75(res$trn, "tmean")   tmax  <- get_75(res$trn, "tmax")   tmin  <- get_75(res$trn, "tmin")   prcp  <- get_75(res$trn, "prcp")   vpd   <- get_75(res$trn, "vpd")   data.frame(     Ecoregion = ecoregions$code[i],     `tmean degC/dec` = round(10 * tmean$slope, 2),     `tmax degC/dec`  = round(10 * tmax$slope, 2),     `tmin degC/dec`  = round(10 * tmin$slope, 2),     `prcp mm/yr` = round(prcp$slope, 3),     `prcp p` = round(prcp$mk_pvalue, 3),     `vpd hPa/dec` = round(10 * vpd$slope, 3),     `vpd p` = round(vpd$mk_pvalue, 3),     `prcp pct change` = round(       cmp$difference[cmp$variable == "prcp" & cmp$period == "annual"], 1     ),     `soil moisture pct change` = round(       cmp$difference[cmp$variable == "soil_moisture" & cmp$period == "annual"], 1     ),     check.names = FALSE   ) })) knitr::kable(rollup, row.names = FALSE,   caption = "Per-ecoregion roll-up over the 75-year window (1951-present): annual mean, daytime maximum, and overnight minimum temperature trends (degrees C per decade); annual precipitation trend (mm per year) and Mann-Kendall p-value; annual vapour pressure deficit trend (hPa per decade) and p-value; and recent (2015-2025) vs pre-warming (1951-1980) percent change for precipitation and soil moisture. All temperature p-values are below 0.001. A tmin slope greater than the tmax slope indicates the textbook day-night asymmetry.")

negative = earlier melt)") +   theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +   theme(legend.position = "bottom") get_75 <- function(trn, var) {   trn[trn$variable == var & trn$period == "annual" & trn$trend_start == 1951, ] } rollup <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(results), function(i) {   res <- results[[i]]   cmp <- res$cmp   tmean <- get_75(res$trn, "tmean")   tmax  <- get_75(res$trn, "tmax")   tmin  <- get_75(res$trn, "tmin")   prcp  <- get_75(res$trn, "prcp")   vpd   <- get_75(res$trn, "vpd")   data.frame(     Ecoregion = ecoregions$code[i],     `tmean degC/dec` = round(10 * tmean$slope, 2),     `tmax degC/dec`  = round(10 * tmax$slope, 2),     `tmin degC/dec`  = round(10 * tmin$slope, 2),     `prcp mm/yr` = round(prcp$slope, 3),     `prcp p` = round(prcp$mk_pvalue, 3),     `vpd hPa/dec` = round(10 * vpd$slope, 3),     `vpd p` = round(vpd$mk_pvalue, 3),     `prcp pct change` = round(       cmp$difference[cmp$variable == "prcp" & cmp$period == "annual"], 1     ),     `soil moisture pct change` = round(       cmp$difference[cmp$variable == "soil_moisture" & cmp$period == "annual"], 1     ),     check.names = FALSE   ) })) knitr::kable(rollup, row.names = FALSE,   caption = "Per-ecoregion roll-up over the 75-year window (1951-present): annual mean, daytime maximum, and overnight minimum temperature trends (degrees C per decade); annual precipitation trend (mm per year) and Mann-Kendall p-value; annual vapour pressure deficit trend (hPa per decade) and p-value; and recent (2015-2025) vs pre-warming (1951-1980) percent change for precipitation and soil moisture. All temperature p-values are below 0.001. A tmin slope greater than the tmax slope indicates the textbook day-night asymmetry.")

title: Climate Departure for the FWCP Peace Region
text: Three findings emerge climate departures shown . Warming broad, fast, uniform. five ecoregions warmed +1.7 °C +1.9 °C cumulative since 1951, rate near +0.3 °C per decade, Mann-Kendall p-values 0.001 every ecoregion. trend statistically significant beyond reasonable doubt random noise. Daily maximum, daily minimum, daily mean temperatures tell story. thermal hot spot. whole region moved together. subtle gradient emerge — northern, higher-elevation ecoregions (Omineca Mountains, Boreal Mountains Plateaus) warmed 0.2 °C southern Fraser Basin, consistent well-documented pattern high-latitude high-elevation amplification — small relative regional signal. Precipitation increasing significantly two northernmost ecoregions. Across FWCP Peace whole, long-term trend annual precipitation statistically significant. Broken ecoregion, Boreal Mountains Plateaus Northern Canadian Rocky Mountains — northern half region — show real increases annual precipitation (p = 0.02 0.01). southern central ecoregions show significant change. north-versus-south contrast regional average hides, place breaking analysis ecoregion worth . Mapping contrast onto watershed-group reporting unit: five watershed groups sit dominated wetter ecoregions — Toodoggone, Firesteel Ingenika sit almost entirely within Boreal Mountains Plateaus, Finlay Fox mostly Northern Canadian Rocky Mountains. , small upward precipitation trend statistically defensible. remaining 13 watershed groups sit elsewhere — Fraser Basin, Central Canadian Rockies, Omineca Mountains — annual precipitation changed way distinguishable natural variability. atmosphere drying despite, places, precipitation. Vapour pressure deficit — gap much water air hold much actually — rose significantly every ecoregion (p < 0.005 across five). Warmer air holds water saturating, pulls moisture soil vegetation evaporation transpiration. Soil moisture essentially flat every ecoregion — even precipitation increased — warmer atmosphere drinking extra water back. headline ecological finding region: water inputs may rising places, soil vegetation seeing available moisture. Snow leaving region earlier, falling less. Annual snowfall across FWCP Peace essentially unchanged (-6%); snowpack story timing, quantity. Spring snowmelt rose 37% (212 → 290 mm) summer snowmelt fell 61% (165 → 64 mm) — total annual melt, redistributed earlier calendar. Annual peak snow water equivalent (SWE) 10% (135 → 122 mm), strikingly, summer SWE collapsed 75%: high-elevation snowpack historically lingered June July longer . direct mechanism behind freshet shift described snow-trend literature: warmer winters springs stop snow falling — shorten long stays ground. freshet-timing signal broadly uniform across region — DOY-50 (day--year half year’s snowmelt happened) shifting earlier roughly 1 day per decade every one five ecoregions, Mann-Kendall p-values 0.01 (median p ≈ 0.005). sixteen FWCP Peace watershed groups, regardless ecoregion sit , seeing order--magnitude earlier melt. peak-SWE signal noisier — small recent-decade declines show every ecoregion, year--year variability large enough long-term Theil-Sen slopes reach statistical significance ecoregion scale. 75% summer-SWE collapse detectable regional aggregate spatial averaging suppresses variability. cold-water resident salmonids FWCP Peace supports — bull trout, Arctic grayling, mountain whitefish, rainbow trout, kokanee — signals compound. Stream temperatures likely rising step warmer ambient air temperatures; evapotranspiration imbalance means low-flow conditions late summer relieved precipitation increase occur; cold-water input high-elevation snowpack provides streams warmest, thermally stressful weeks summer dropping parallel summer SWE. spring freshet — dominant high-flow event shapes channel morphology, mobilizes spawning gravels, refills -channel rearing habitat — shifting weeks earlier; neighbouring Fraser Basin documents kind freshet advance (Kang et al. 2016) comparable magnitude.

title: Climate Departure Analysis from ERA5-Land Reanalysis
text: cd fetches ERA5-Land hourly reanalysis (1950–present, ~9 km native grid) DestinE Earth Data Hub, subsets British Columbia, aggregates monthly, seasonal, annual periods, derives additional variables (vapour pressure deficit relative humidity temperature + dewpoint via Tetens equation, soil moisture 4-depth mean), writes Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFFs alongside static SpatioTemporal Asset Catalog (STAC) public S3 bucket. monthly GitHub Action keeps catalog current. consumer side, R functions (cd_catalog, cd_extract, cd_baseline, cd_anomaly, cd_trend, cd_compare, cd_summary) read COGs directly via GDAL’s /vsicurl/ — credentials, tile server — crop user-supplied area interest, compute baselines anomalies arbitrary reference periods, run Mann-Kendall Theil-Sen trend statistics. baseline comparison logic stays consumer side, reference periods baked served data.

code: library(cd)  # Load the live STAC catalog and an example area of interest catalog <- cd_catalog() aoi <- sf::st_read(   system.file("extdata", "example_aoi_kotl.gpkg", package = "cd"),   quiet = TRUE )  # Extract zonal mean time series ts <- cd_extract(catalog, aoi)  # Compute baseline and anomalies bl <- cd_baseline(ts, baseline_years = 1951:1955) ano <- cd_anomaly(ts, bl)  # Trend analysis trn <- cd_trend(ano, trend_start = 1951)  # Reporting table cd_summary(trn)  # Compare time windows directly cd_compare(ts, window_a = 1956:1960, window_b = 1951:1955)

Catalog (JSON): https://stac-era5-land.s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/catalog.json Region: BC (~48–60° N, 114–140° W), 1950–2025, ~9 km native grid Core climate (7): tmean, tmax, tmin, prcp, vpd, rh, soil_moisture Snow monthly natives (4): swe, snowfall, snowmelt, snow_cover Snow annual derived (4): swe_max, snowfall_fraction, snowmelt_doy_50, snowmelt_rate_peak Periods: seasonal (DJF/MAM/JJA/SON) annual monthly-native vars; annual snow_max / snowfall_fraction / snowmelt_doy_50 / snowmelt_rate_peak catalog consumable directly outside R — example, QGIS via STAC plugin, gdalcubes, STAC-aware client. cd_catalog() consumer function reads catalog URL default.

title: cd 0.2.0 (2026-05-04)
text: Adds 8 snow-related variables hydrology departure analysis. Four monthly natives (swe, snowfall, snowmelt, snow_cover) ship 12-band/year COGs alongside existing 7 climate variables; four annual derived scalars (swe_max, snowfall_fraction, snowmelt_doy_50, snowmelt_rate_peak) ship 1-band/year COGs. New pct_point_diff anomaly type cd_anomaly() snow_cover snowfall_fraction (already-percentage variables). New “Snowpack” section FWCP Peace vignette seasonal-curve table, four annual time-series plots, per-ecoregion view, citation-grounded interpretation; ASWS QA cross-check 4 BC stations confirms bias stable time even absolute values biased. Producer pipeline extends scripts/backfill_edh_snow.py (first place ECMWF’s accumulation-reset trick lands code). Also bug-fix cd_stac_item() filename parsing (substring match mis-routing variables whose names contain variable names — swe_max filed swe). Headline finding: regional summer SWE collapsed 75% record, freshet-timing shift uniform across 5 ecoregions ~1 day/decade earlier melt. (#55)

title: cd 0.1.7 (2026-05-04)
text: Snow-methodology literature review upcoming “Snowpack” vignette section. Adds scripts/rag_build_snow_methodology.R scripts/rag_query_snow_methodology.R, builds local ragnar DuckDB 11 peer-reviewed papers (now NewGraphEnvironment/hydrology Zotero collection), ships methodology synthesis + 15-row “cite ” citation map planning/archive/2026-05-issue-53-snow-lit-review/findings.md. Headline finding: cd_trend()’s raw Mann-Kendall + Theil-Sen (prewhitening) methodologically correct 76-year series strong trends per Yue & Wang 2002 — prewhitening underestimate slope real trend exists. (#54)

title: cd 0.1.6 (2026-05-03)
text: Producer-side refactor. Extracted shared safeguards (single-instance pgrep guard, exponential-backoff retry, atomic GeoTIFF write, timestamped logging, EDH token loader) scripts/backfill_edh_all.py new scripts/_lib.py, ported sibling scripts/backfill_edh_tmax_tmin.py (previously none). Adds backup_before_delete() helper codifying -disk pattern data/backfill/monthly/_cds_backup/. consumer-side changes; sets planned snow-variables backfill (#48) inherit safeguards via single import. (#52)

title: cd 0.1.5 (2026-05-02)
text: Adds “Watershed Groups Across Ecoregions” section peace-fwcp vignette: map 16 canonical FWCP Peace watershed groups labelled codes top ecoregion fills, plus table showing percent watershed group’s area falling five ecoregions. Lets readers map per-ecoregion climate departure findings (precipitation BMP NRM) onto watershed-group reporting unit. Canonical 16-WSG list (CARP, CRKD, FINA, FINL, FIRE, FOXR, INGR, LOMI, MESI, NATR, OSPK, PARA, PARS, PCEA, TOOD, UOMI) hardcoded data-raw/example_context_fwcp_peace.R reuse — UPCE MURR dropped sit mostly outside FWCP boundary. Recent vs Pre-warming consolidated one table. six vignette tables now render single, clean bookdown captions (label = NA + caption = "..." pattern). (#47)

title: cd 0.1.4 (2026-05-01)
text: KOTL vignette removed. peace-fwcp now canonical worked example — covers everything KOTL higher fidelity (regional + per-ecoregion + day-night asymmetry + plain-language explainers + interpretation), dropping second live-S3 vignette saves ~60 s per pkgdown render removes last /vsicurl/ flake surface doc build. KOTL polygon assets stay inst/extdata/ README quick-start still uses . Future direction (single-vignette snowpack story split vignettes AOI scale) deferred snow-pack variables land — see #49. (#50)
